title: Race-control and tyre-rule finite-state expert
summary: Official FIA rules make dry-race compound obligations path-dependent, and FastF1 exposes weather plus track-status channels including Safety Car and VSC.
rationale: Official FIA rules make dry-race compound obligations path-dependent, and FastF1 exposes weather plus track-status channels including Safety Car and VSC. Those events materially change both whether a stop is strategically cheap and whether a driver still faces mandatory compound pressure, which the current feature set only approximates.
implementation_hint: Join FastF1 race-level weather, rainfall, and track-status timelines to laps; create a finite-state machine tracking dry/wet legality, mandatory dry-compound obligation, Monaco special rule, laps since SC/VSC onset, and 'cheap stop' windows under neutralization. Use these features in a small specialist model or as gating variables for the main model.
expected_effect: Should improve rare but high-leverage laps where neutralizations or wet-track exemptions dominate tyre-life logic.
risk: External joins can be brittle across sessions and years; alignment errors will silently hurt CV.
